skill.name: rag-engineer
skill.description: Guidance for designing Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems, including chunking, embeddings, vector search, hybrid retrieval, reranking, and evaluation.
skill.summary: Design practical RAG pipelines with better retrieval quality and evaluation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown backticks and retrieval-search terminology; no executable code, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. This line is prose inside SKILL.md and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line opens a Markdown code fence. The surrounding content is a bullet list about semantic chunking, not executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Boundary Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. This line is prose inside SKILL.md and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line closes a Markdown code fence. There is no shell invocation, interpreter directive, or user input path.

content.value_statement: RAG teams often struggle to turn documents into accurate LLM context. This skill guides chunking, embeddings, retrieval, reranking, and evaluation.
actual_capabilities: Design retrieval-augmented generation pipelines for document-heavy LLM applications.
Recommend semantic chunking approaches that preserve headings, paragraphs, and context.
Compare embedding, vector search, keyword search, and hybrid retrieval options.
Plan reranking, metadata filtering, and context selection for higher precision.
Define retrieval evaluation steps separate from answer quality checks.
limitations: Provides architecture guidance, not a complete production implementation.
Does not include scripts, connectors, or vector database setup files.
Needs project details to choose models, indexes, and evaluation metrics.
Cannot verify private documents or live retrieval quality without access.

use_cases.title: Plan a Knowledge Base RAG System
use_cases.description: Map documents, chunking rules, vector indexes, filters, and evaluation checks before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Quality
use_cases.description: Diagnose weak retrieval results and choose hybrid search, reranking, or metadata filters.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Retrieval Separately
use_cases.description: Create a review plan that measures recall, precision, freshness, and context relevance.

prompt_templates.title: Start a RAG Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new document assistant.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a RAG pipeline for these document types, user questions, and quality goals. Ask for missing details first.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Chunking Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of document structure and retrieval needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend a chunking strategy for my documents. Consider headings, sentence boundaries, overlap, metadata, and update frequency.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Retrieval Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced optimization for an existing system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current retrieval approach and suggest changes for hybrid search, reranking, filtering, and context selection.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Evaluation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a retrieval evaluation plan with test queries, relevance labels, failure categories, and separate generation checks.

output_examples.input: A team needs a RAG plan for policy documents with frequent updates.
output_examples.output: A staged architecture plan covering semantic chunking, metadata filters, embedding refresh, hybrid search, reranking, and retrieval metrics.

output_examples.input: A support assistant returns broad results and misses exact product terms.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis that recommends keyword and vector fusion, query-type routing, metadata prefilters, and precision-focused reranking.

output_examples.input: A product manager wants to know if retrieval is improving.
output_examples.output: An evaluation checklist with labeled queries, recall targets, freshness checks, and a separate answer-quality review.

best_practices: Evaluate retrieval quality before judging generated answers.
Use metadata filters and reranking when semantic search alone is too broad.
Refresh embeddings when source documents change materially.
anti_patterns: Using fixed chunk sizes that split sentences or sections.
Putting every retrieved passage into the prompt without relevance thresholds.
Using one retrieval strategy for every query type and content source.

faq.question: Does this skill build a full RAG application?
faq.answer: No. It provides design and review guidance for RAG systems, but it does not ship application code.

faq.question: Can it help choose an embedding model?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare options based on content type, language needs, cost, latency, and evaluation results.

faq.question: Does it support hybrid search design?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers keyword search, vector search, metadata filtering, and score fusion patterns.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing RAG system?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review chunking, retrieval, reranking, context selection, and evaluation gaps.

faq.question: Does it require a specific vector database?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is database-neutral and can be adapted to common vector search platforms.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found documentation-only content and no executable code, network behavior, or prompt injection.
